meta.title: Analog compute-in-memory architecture learning data
meta.scope: Mythic's publicly disclosed flash-based inference architecture, placed beside representative PCM, RRAM, SRAM, and hybrid CIM systems.
meta.terminology: The literature most often uses AIMC or AiMC for analog in-memory computing. ACIM and analog CIM are also common. This study treats the user's AiCM term as analog compute-in-memory.

signalPath.label: Model preparation
signalPath.summary: Convert the trained floating-point graph into a precision and operator set the target can execute.
signalPath.detail: Mythic describes a post-training path from FP32 to an 8-bit analog representation called ANA8, with quantization-aware and analog-aware retraining available for sensitive models. This step decides clipping ranges, scales, supported operators, and where digital fallback is required.
signalPath.failureMode: Naive quantization can amplify outliers or make a sensitive layer lose accuracy before any analog error is added.
signalPath.mitigation: Calibrate on representative data, use per-channel scales where supported, and apply quantization-aware or analog-aware retraining to sensitive layers.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Explain why analog-aware training is not the same as ordinary INT8 quantization-aware training.

signalPath.label: Graph mapping
signalPath.summary: Partition matrices across fixed-size arrays, place digital operators, allocate buffers, and schedule data movement.
signalPath.detail: The graph compiler converts a logical A[B,K] x W[K,N] operation into repeated or parallel MVMs. It packs W blocks into arrays, partitions K into partial-sum reductions, partitions N into output concatenations, chooses sign and bit-slice representations, places digital operators, allocates buffers, and emits code for local control and routing. Array utilization matters because a half-empty array still pays much of its converter and peripheral cost.
signalPath.failureMode: Poor tiling increases unused rows and columns, partial-sum traffic, conversion count, or off-chip communication.
signalPath.mitigation: Use shape-aware packing, operator fusion, layer pipelining, explicit buffer allocation, and an end-to-end cost model that includes converters and the network on chip.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Map a 3072 by 4096 projection onto 1024 by 1024 arrays and identify the required partial sums and traffic.

signalPath.label: Input encoding
signalPath.summary: Represent each digital activation as a voltage, current, pulse width, or bit-serial sequence.
signalPath.detail: An ideal description uses a DAC for each input row. Mythic's 2018 Hot Chips presentation said its first implementation used digital approximation instead of a full input DAC. Other AIMC chips commonly use bit-serial pulses or duration coding to trade latency for precision and simpler drivers.
signalPath.failureMode: Driver mismatch, limited voltage headroom, timing jitter, and additional input cycles distort the intended activation.
signalPath.mitigation: Calibrate row drivers, segment high-precision inputs, limit simultaneous row activation, and include input encoding in the precision and energy model.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Compare spatial bit slicing with temporal bit-serial input encoding.

signalPath.label: Weight storage
signalPath.summary: Program each neural-network weight into one or more non-volatile memory conductances.
signalPath.detail: Mythic uses embedded flash cells. The cell threshold is programmed so its read current acts like a tunable conductance. PCM and RRAM systems use different physical state variables but expose the same system abstraction: a programmed conductance that represents a weight.
signalPath.failureMode: Programming error, device-to-device variation, stuck cells, finite conductance range, relaxation, drift, and retention change the stored value.
signalPath.mitigation: Use iterative write-verify, defect maps, differential or offset encoding, spare cells, multi-cell weights, recalibration, and selective refresh.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Why can a device have many measurable conductance levels but still provide fewer reliable weight bits in a full array?

signalPath.label: Analog dot product
signalPath.summary: Use Ohm's law for multiplication and current or charge summation for accumulation.
signalPath.detail: For an input voltage V_i and cell conductance G_ij, the memory cell itself contributes approximately I_ij = V_i G_ij. The column network and sensing circuit combine those contributions, giving I_j approximately equal to the sum over i of V_i G_ij. This is the bulk multiply-accumulate: the host and tile RISC-V schedule the operation but do not execute one instruction per cell MAC. ADC and digital reconstruction are still required before the result becomes a framework tensor value.
signalPath.failureMode: Wire resistance, source resistance, sneak paths, nonlinear I-V behavior, limited headroom, coupling, and accumulated noise break the ideal linear equation.
signalPath.mitigation: Use smaller arrays, lower read voltage, access transistors, IR-drop-aware mapping, reference paths, calibrated operating points, and algorithmic error tolerance.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Derive the ideal matrix-vector multiply, then name every assumption that fails in a large physical array.

signalPath.label: Readout and ADC
signalPath.summary: Convert an analog column result into a bounded digital code for the rest of the system.
signalPath.detail: The ADC sets an important effective-precision ceiling. More ADC bits can reduce quantization error, but converter area and energy can dominate the array. Practical chips use limited-resolution ADCs, range scaling, multiple read phases, or adaptive thresholds.
signalPath.failureMode: Offset, gain error, saturation, quantization, reference noise, and column mismatch create structured output error.
signalPath.mitigation: Apply per-column gain and offset calibration, choose layer-specific ranges, use adaptive quantization, average or expand sensitive weights, and reserve higher precision for sensitive layers.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Why does adding two ADC bits not automatically add two useful bits to the end-to-end result?

signalPath.label: Local digital work
signalPath.summary: Handle nonlinear, control-heavy, stateful, and precision-sensitive work near the analog array.
signalPath.detail: The disclosed M1076 tile combines the analog compute engine with SRAM, a 16-bit SIMD vector processor, a 32-bit RISC-V nano-processor, and a network-on-chip router. The public brief establishes those blocks but does not publish their firmware ABI or exact per-operator ownership. Architecturally, the RISC-V is suited to coarse sequencing and control, SRAM and the router to activation/result movement, the ACE to bulk MVM, and SIMD or fixed digital logic to slice reconstruction, partial-sum reduction, vector epilogues, and unsupported array operations.
signalPath.failureMode: Amdahl's law appears when digital post-processing, attention, routing, or underused tiles dominate end-to-end time and energy.
signalPath.mitigation: Fuse operations, increase local reuse, route precision-sensitive kernels to efficient digital units, and report sustained system metrics rather than array-only TOPS/W.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Which transformer operations belong in analog arrays, and which should remain digital? Defend the boundary.

signalPath.label: Tile dataflow
signalPath.summary: Pipeline graph nodes across tiles and move activations instead of repeatedly moving weights.
signalPath.detail: Mythic describes assigning graph nodes and stationary weights to compute-in-memory arrays, then routing ready activations to the local compute and memory. At runtime, activations and outputs move through SRAM and the NoC while weights remain programmed. This can make batch-one latency predictable, but K-partition reductions, N-shard fanout, bit phases, tile utilization, converter sharing, intermediate buffers, and network traffic determine sustained performance.
signalPath.failureMode: Imbalanced stages, routing congestion, oversized intermediate tensors, and model shapes that do not fit the tile grid reduce utilization.
signalPath.mitigation: Pipeline layers, overlap communication with compute, balance tile assignments, place communicating stages nearby, and profile the complete graph at batch one.
signalPath.interviewPrompt: Explain when weight-stationary dataflow wins and when activation or partial-sum traffic becomes the new bottleneck.

mythicSnapshot.label: Current roadmap claim
mythicSnapshot.evidence: Vendor claim; no public independent reproduction found

mythicSnapshot.label: 2026 direction
mythicSnapshot.context: Analog matrix work plus unified digital compute

technologyComparison.inputOutput: Historical disclosure uses digital input approximation and ADC readout
technologyComparison.bestFit: Batch-one edge inference with stationary weights
technologyComparison.mainCaveat: Public end-to-end accuracy and current roadmap details are mostly vendor disclosed

technologyComparison.weightRole: One or more PCM devices per weight
technologyComparison.inputOutput: Duration or phased input, current integration, on-chip ADC and digital auxiliaries
technologyComparison.bestFit: Research-scale multicore inference, including speech and transformer studies
technologyComparison.mainCaveat: Drift and noise require hardware-aware training, expansion, and recalibration

technologyComparison.bestFit: Reconfigurable CNN, LSTM, and probabilistic edge workloads
technologyComparison.mainCaveat: Board-level FPGA handled buffers and partial sums in the reported prototype

technologyComparison.weightRole: Layer-sensitive split between dense non-volatile and precise volatile paths
technologyComparison.inputOutput: INT and FP modes with tiny digital units
technologyComparison.bestFit: High-accuracy edge inference under heterogeneous precision
technologyComparison.mainCaveat: Accuracy is preserved partly by moving sensitive work away from pure analog compute

technologyComparison.weightRole: Volatile bits in standard-like memory cells
technologyComparison.inputOutput: Charge, current, or digital bit-line operations
technologyComparison.bestFit: Frequently updated weights and easier CMOS integration
technologyComparison.mainCaveat: Lower density and static leakage; weights must be loaded after power-up
